actions: Read external/README.md (full 283 lines) — PRAT references: algorithm name (line 29 'Powered by the PRAT (Persistent Recursive Abstract Tree) algorithm'), indexing model (line 240 'Xanther CLI or GitHub App indexes your repo into a knowledge graph'), trace chain (lines 215-217 'function → class → module → architectural role'), HLD/LLD mentions (lines 24,136,206-207), architecture diagram with PRAT + Graph Store (lines 229-238), benchmark numbers (lines 37-47, +7.4pp SWE-bench), token reduction claim (line 188, ~20%)
Read code-graph-db.ts:107-160 — SQLite schema: content_hash (line 111), CONTAINS edges (lines 138-145), code_graph_metadata KV store (lines 151-155)
Read code-graph-db.ts:755-822 — isFileStale() content-hash-keyed freshness gate + ensureFreshFiles() batch stale check
Read indexer-types.ts:1-198 — SymbolKind hierarchy (lines 12-15), EdgeType CONTAINS (line 19), generateContentHash SHA-256 12-hex (lines 117-120), ParseResult fields (lines 79-89)
Read structural-indexer.ts:1406-1463 — recursive walk() directory descent with maxDepth and node-limit guards
Read structural-indexer.ts:944-993 — capturesToNodes() converts AST captures to CodeNode[] with module hierarchy
Read structural-indexer.ts:2077-2149 — skipFreshFiles flag (line 2083) + isFileStale() integration (line 2140) for incremental indexing
Read tree-sitter-parser.ts:1-60 — web-tree-sitter WASM cursor-based AST walk producing RawCapture[]
Read iterations 001-004 — F-003 gap analysis, F-005 HLD/LLD proposal, F-008 CONTAINS edges, F-012 trace pipeline, F-016 risk signals, F-021 budget model
verdict: DEFER (full pipeline) / ADAPT (Persistent, Recursive, Tree) / ADOPT (Abstract)
summary: RQ5: PRAT decomposed into 4 stages with external/README.md evidence per stage. Persistent → ADAPT (isFileStale at code-graph-db.ts:755, skipFreshFiles at structural-indexer.ts:2083). Recursive → ADAPT (walk() at structural-indexer.ts:1406, tree-sitter-parser.ts AST walk, CONTAINS edges at indexer-types.ts:19). Abstract → ADOPT (gap — no narrative layer; proposed code-graph-hld-lld.ts ~200 LOC template-only). Tree → ADAPT (tree-sitter WASM + capturesToNodes() + SymbolKind hierarchy). Full PRAT pipeline DEFER (closed-source SaaS). Cheap approximation: ~370 LOC total on existing infrastructure with zero new dependencies.

label: PRAT name decomposed: Persistent (content-hash-keyed indexing — external/README.md:240), Recursive (tree-walk + upward CONTAINS traversal — external/README.md:215-217), Abstract (HLD/LLD component descriptions — external/README.md:24,136,206-207), Tree (AST + module hierarchy — external/README.md:212,240). All 4 evidenced in external/README.md.

evidence.quote: Powered by the PRAT (Persistent Recursive Abstract Tree) algorithm.
Xanther CLI or GitHub App indexes your repo into a knowledge graph
Source: 'validate_token', Target: 'hld' → Returns: function → class → module → architectural role
Returns: what it does, how it fits in the architecture, what depends on it
XCE provides HLD (high-level design), LLD (low-level design), call graphs, and component descriptions

verdict: DEFER (full pipeline) / ADAPT (3 of 4 stages) / ADOPT (Abstract stage)

label: PRAT stage-to-local-file map: Persistent → code-graph-db.ts:755-773 (isFileStale content-hash freshness, MEDIUM confidence, inferred). Recursive → structural-indexer.ts:1406-1463 (walk() directory descent) + tree-sitter-parser.ts:1-60 (AST walk) + indexer-types.ts:19 (CONTAINS edges, HIGH confidence, documented). Abstract → NO EQUIVALENT (gap, HIGH confidence, documented). Tree → tree-sitter-parser.ts (WASM AST) + capturesToNodes() (structural-indexer.ts:944) + SymbolKind hierarchy (indexer-types.ts:12-15, MEDIUM confidence, inferred).

evidence.quote: isFileStale: content_hash comparison to skip re-parsing unchanged files
recursive walk() with maxDepth guard, gitignore inheritance, node-limit abort
web-tree-sitter WASM cursor-based AST walk producing RawCapture[]
'CONTAINS' in EdgeType enum with 1.0 default weight at line 24

verdict: ADAPT (3 stages existing) / ADOPT (Abstract stage new)

label: Cheapness estimate: ~370 total LOC to approximate all 4 PRAT stages on top of existing structural-indexer + tree-sitter + code-graph-db. Breakdown: HLD/LLD template generation ~200 LOC (code-graph-hld-lld.ts new), recursive memoization ~50 LOC (code-graph-db.ts edit), version-tagged snapshots ~70 LOC (code-graph-db.ts edit), code_packages table ~50 LOC (code-graph-db.ts edit). Zero new dependencies. Template-only HLD/LLD has zero runtime cost. LLM enrichment additive.

evidence.quote: F-005: Minimum-viable deterministic HLD/LLD schema from existing graph data — file_role from kind counts, layer from import/export ratios, summary from template strings
isFileStale already provides content-hash-keyed freshness — no new persistence needed for basic caching
code_edges stores CONTAINS edges — recursive upward walk already supported via queryEdgesTo(CONTAINS)

verdict: ADAPT — ~370 LOC across 4 edits for near-complete PRAT approximation

label: Benchmark numbers analysis: +7.4pp SWE-bench for Sonnet 4.0 + XCE (external/README.md:41). Cascade hybrid +10.8pp (line 43) suggests multi-pass context strategy. Diminishing return on MiniMax M2.5 (+2.4pp vs +7.4pp) suggests PRAT's Abstract stage is most valuable for weaker models. ~20% token reduction (line 188) attributed to steering, not PRAT directly.

evidence.quote: Sonnet 4.0 baseline 66.0% → +XCE 73.4% (+7.4pp). Cascade hybrid 76.8% (+10.8pp). MiniMax M2.5 baseline 75.8% → +XCE 78.2% (+2.4pp).
~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively

verdict: BENCHMARK EVIDENCE — consistent with PRAT providing architectural narrative context, strongest benefit on weaker models
